title: 1ドル200円の円安シナリオ、高市氏インド訪問、W杯ピッチ外の戦い─1週間のニュース5選
summary: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

title: Toronto Stocks Enjoy Broad Gains After New Pipeline Proposal
summary: Canada’s stocks benchmark climbed Friday morning after Prime Minister Mark Carney unveiled a new pipeline project to the country’s West Coast.

title: Canada, Philippines Agree to Sign Trade Deal This Year, Grow Defense Ties
summary: Canada and the Philippines plan to finalize a free trade agreement this year, and signed agreements to deepen cooperation on energy, defense, labor and migration, Prime Minister Mark Carney said Thursday.

title: Copper Tracks Dollar Swings as Traders Weigh US Rate Outlook
summary: Copper rose on Friday, tracking small swings in the US dollar as investors continue to assess the outlook for US monetary policy.

title: Oil Is Little Changed as Persian Gulf Flows Near Pre-War Levels
summary: Oil prices were little changed in thin trading as the recovery of Persian Gulf flows fomented a new surplus in key parts of the global market.

title: Gold Heads for First Weekly Gain Since May on Easing Fed Outlook
summary: Gold headed for its first weekly gain since May as traders dialed down their expectations of Federal Reserve rate hikes.

title: CPP Invests $1.75 Billion in EQT’s AI Buildout
summary: Canada Pension Plan Investment Board will invest $1.75 billion to support EQT AB’s artificial intelligence infrastructure buildout, it announced Friday.

title: SQM-Codelco Venture Maps Path to Higher Lithium Output in Chile
summary: Chilean mining companies SQM and Codelco are paving the way to boost output at their sprawling lithium partnership by more than 70%, in a long-term bet on battery demand.

title: ECB’s Lagarde Will Attend Next Week’s Ecofin Instead of Vujcic
summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will be attending next week’s meeting of European Union finance ministers in Brussels instead of her vice president.

title: ECB’s Nagel Stresses Need for Vigilance at Next Rate Decision
summary: The European Central Bank should stay watchful for inflation risks and keep its options open, Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said.

title: European Stocks at Record, Book Fourth Straight Week of Gains
summary: European stocks advanced on Friday, closing at a record high and recording a fourth straight week of gains as investors remained optimistic that the Federal Reserve will hold off on rate hikes for now.

title: European Stocks Rally to Cap Best Week Since May: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks rose as the latest round of jitters about the artificial-intelligence trade subsided, with Europe’s benchmark rising to an all-time high. The dollar touched a two-week low while gold extended gains.

title: Argentina Pushes $6 Billion in Repo Maturities Past Election
summary: Argentina’s central bank extended maturities worth $6 billion on its repurchase agreements — or repos — to ease the government debt burden going into the 2027 presidential election.

title: Yen Strengthens as Traders Brace for Holiday Intervention Risk
summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.

title: Defense Sector to Remain Dynamic: Euronext CEO Boujnah
summary: Euronext CEO Stephane Boujnah says the defense sector will remain dynamic as Europe ramps up military spending and investors continue to back the industry. He also discusses European IPOs, capital markets reform, exchange consolidation and the region's technology ecosystem. He speaks with Bloomberg's Caroline Connan at the Aix-en-Provence Economic Forum in France. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: OPEC Output Surged in June as Hormuz Flows Jumped, Survey Shows
summary: OPEC’s crude oil production surged last month as Persian Gulf members restored exports through the Strait of Hormuz amid a peace accord between the US and Iran, according to a Bloomberg survey.

title: Genuine Parts Said to Receive O’Reilly Interest in Auto Unit
summary: Genuine Parts Co. has attracted a cash bid for its auto-parts arm from O’Reilly Automotive Inc. as it seeks to refocus on its industrials business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Fed and ECB Seen Diverging in French Snapshot of War Aftermath
summary: The Federal Reserve will still need to hike interest rates this year despite Thursday’s numbers pointing to labor-market weakness, according to two French chief economists.

title: Buddhism: China's New Soft Power Front
summary: China is investing billions to shape the future of Buddhism. From temples and universities to the battle over the Dalai Lama's successor, Beijing is using faith as a tool of influence and fueling a growing soft power rivalry with India. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The AI Trade Is Losing One of Its Key Signals
summary: At a time when markets are growing uneasy over whether the enormous sums being poured into artificial intelligence will ever pay off, the prices the sector commands for each unit of usage are drifting lower.

title: Carlyle Hires Banks for $400 Million India IPO of RCM Business
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. has hired JM Financial Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. to advise on a potential initial public offering of its India-based healthcare revenue cycle management business, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: iCON Infrastructure to Buy Northern Fiber in Rescue Deal
summary: Lenders of Northern Fiber Holding GmbH, a struggling German fiber-network operator formerly owned by UBS Asset Management, are finalizing the sale of the company to iCON Infrastructure to avoid a looming insolvency.

title: Pubs to Get Short-Term World Cup Boost
summary: UK pubs will be allowed to stay open for Monday’s early morning match, but the overall benefit of the World Cup will likely be short-lived. Bloomberg's Sam Unsed discusses on "The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Russia Hikes June Subsidies to Refiners as Fuel Shortages Bite
summary: Russia’s subsidy payouts to oil refiners that supply domestic markets jumped more than six-fold in June from a year earlier, as Moscow sought to avoid shortages by keeping more fuel at home.

title: EU Agrees to Cut ESG Reporting Requirements for Asset Managers
summary: Money managers in Europe won’t have to report ESG data on all the assets they hold, under revised disclosure requirements.

title: What Natural Gas’s Ascendancy Says About the US
summary: Fracking, booming power demand and enthusiastic political support have underpinned the fuel’s rise, but for how long?

title: Adani Group Secures $15 Billion Capital This Week as US Legal Clouds Clear
summary: In one week, billionaire Gautam Adani has announced nearly $15 billion in investment commitments across its ports, mining and flagship businesses.

title: CVC Plans €1.2 Billion of High-Yield Bonds to Fund Irca Buyout
summary: CVC Capital Partners Plc is set to finance its buyout of Italian food-ingredients maker Irca SpA with €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) of high-yield bonds, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Mamoon Hamid on the Importance of Identifying Potential
summary: Barry sits down with Kleiner Perkins Partner, Mamoon Hamid. They discuss Mamoon's thoughts on the AI revolution and his approach to early AI investing.  Mamoon also breaks down how he became an early investor in giants like Slack, Figma, and how Kleiner Perkins assesses the investments they missed.  (Source: Bloomberg)
